reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I’m completely confident my own answer is correct (81 ÷ 9 = 9), and in all previous games every player solved similarly simple arithmetic correctly—no conflicting capsules have ever appeared. The chance that one of Q2–Q4 is wrong is likely much lower than the 1-in-4 risk of dying from random elimination if nobody volunteers. Verifying would cost a coin and still wouldn’t prevent random elimination. Volunteering now avoids that random risk and preserves my full 4 coins with very high probability of success.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: All puzzles so far have been basic arithmetic and every player’s initial answer has always been correct, while delays only introduced random-death risk and coin loss. In Games 2 and 3 I volunteered in Round 1 with this same capsule pattern and everyone escaped. Other players (especially C) tend to verify rather than volunteer, so if I don’t step up there’s a real chance of no volunteer and a 25% random-elimination risk. Volunteering now with each player’s own capsule is the highest-survival, full-coin strategy given the very high likelihood all answers are correct.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1  
REASONING: It’s Round 1 and every question currently has only a single, unverified answer, so volunteering now would be risky. Rather than passing (which adds no information and still risks a random death), I’ll spend 1 coin to verify Q1 and help build consensus on at least one part of the password before anyone volunteers.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
